alt: Aerial view of central Miraflores showing the staggered adobe-brick platforms of the Huaca Pucllana pre-Columbian pyramid in the lower right, completely surrounded by the streets and apartment buildings of modern Lima, with the Óvalo Gutiérrez traffic circle visible in the upper left
description: Aerial photograph showing the seven-platform adobe pyramid Huaca Pucllana sitting in the heart of modern Miraflores, surrounded by apartment blocks and the Óvalo Gutiérrez traffic circle. Built by the Lima Culture between 200 and 700 AD; the pre-Columbian site sits a few blocks from the cliffs above the Pacific.
photographer: Evelyn Merino Reyna Buchanan (original photo for Lima Más Arriba); published by Municipalidad de Miraflores
title: Espectacular vista aérea de la Huaca Pucllana y el Óvalo Gutiérrez
